content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
October 31,
1994
Wayne K.
Lemieux, Jr.
Law Offices of Wayne K.
Lemieux
200 North Westlake Blvd.,
Suite 100
Westlake Village,
CA
91362—3722
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-327
Dear Mr.
Lemieux:
This is in response to your request for advice on behalf of
the Las Virgenes Municipal Water District under the mass mailing
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’! This letter
will also serve to confirm telephone advice which you received
from this office.
OUESTION
Is it permissible for the Las Virgenes Municipal Water
District (“district”) to utilize either a header or a footer in
its quarterly newsletter which contains a roster listing of all of
the elected directors of the district?
CONCLUSION
Use of either a footer or header containing a roster listing
of all elected directors is permissible provided the names of all
directors are displayed in the same typeface, style, color and
location.
FACTS
The district publishes a quarterly newsletter titled the
“Current Flow Newsletter.”
The district is desirous of listing
the names of all of its directors in a roster on the newsletter.
The district is proposing to place the roster either in the header
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Coinmission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000—18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804.0807 •
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—94—327
Page
2
on the first page of the newsletter or as a footer on the last
page in the newsletter.
You provided a sample of the two
proposals.
ANAI,YSIS
In June 1988 Proposition 73 amended Section 89001 of the Act
to provide:
“No newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at
public expense.”
The Commission adopted Regulation 18901 to
clarify which mailings were permissible and which were prohibited
under the Act.
2
’
Regulation 18901(a) provides that a mailing is prohibited
only if all of the following apply:
(1)
Any item sent is delivered,
by any means,
to the recipient at his or her residence, place of
employment or business,
or post office box.
For
purposes of this subdivision
(a) (1), the item
delivered to the recipient must be a tangible item,
such as a videotape, record, or button,
or a
written document.
(2)
The item sent either:
(A)
Features an elected officer
affiliated with the agency which produces or
sends the mailing,
or
(B)
Includes the name, office,
photograph,
or other reference to an elected
officer affiliated with the agency which
produces or sends the mailing,
and is prepared
or sent in cooperation, consultation,
coordination,
or concert with the elected
officer.
(3) (A)
Any of the costs of distribution is
paid for with public moneys; or
(B)
Costs of design, production,
and
printing exceeding $50.00 are paid with public
moneys,
and the design, production,
or
printing is done with the intent of sending
the item other than as permitted by this
regulation.
2/
The Commission’s authority to interpret Section 89001 to avoid
the absurd results of its literal application was upheld in
Watson v. Fair Political Practices Com.
(1990)
217 Cal.App.3d
1059.


File No.
A—94—327
Page
3
(4)
More than two hundred substantially
similar items are sent,
in a single calendar month,
excluding any item sent in response to an
unsolicited request and any item described in
subdivision (b).
Regulation 18901(a).
Thus,
a mass mailing is prohibited if all of the factors set
out in Regulation 18901(a)
are met.
If the factors are not met,
the mailing is not a “mass mailing” for purposes of Section 89001.
Clearly, the newsletter which the district sends is a mass mailing
within the meaning of Section 89001.
Regulation 18901(b) lists several exceptions to the
prohibition of Section 89001.
One such exception is any item in
which the elected officer’s name appears only in the letterhead or
logotype of the stationery, or in a roster listing containing the
names of all elected officers of the agency.
In any such item,
the names of all elected officers must appear in the same type
size, typeface, color,
and location.
Such item may not include
the elected officer’s photograph, signature, or any other
reference to the elected officer.
Regulation 18901(b) (1).
The two samples you provided which the district proposes to
use contain the names of all elected officers in a roster listing
in the same typeface, typesize, color and location.
Since both of
the samples meet the exception outlined above, the district would
not be violating Section 89001 if it uses either of the two
samples provided in its quarterly newsletter.
I believe this confirms our telephone conversation and
answers your question sufficiently.
If you need further
assistance, please feel free to contact the Commission’s Legal
Division at
(916)
322—5901.
Sincerely,
Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division
SGC/JET/jt

Is it permissible for the Las Virgenes Municipal Water
District (“district”) to utilize either a header or a footer in
its quarterly newsletter which contains a roster listing of all of
the elected directors of the district?

of all elected directors is permissible provided the names of all
directors are displayed in the same typeface, style, color and
location.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is it permissible for the Las Virgenes Municipal Water
District (“district”) to utilize either a header or a footer in
its quarterly newsletter which contains a roster listing of all of
the elected directors of the district?

CONCLUSION: Use of either a footer or header containing a roster listing
of all elected directors is permissible provided the names of all
directors are displayed in the same typeface, style, color and
location.
